Group A hospitals
description: General hospitals providing facilities for giving instruction to medical students of any university, as evidenced by a written agreement between the hospital and the university with which it is affiliated, and hospitals approved in writing by the Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons for providing post-graduate education leading to certification or a fellowship in one or more of the specialties recognized by the Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons.

name: Group B hospitals
description: General hospitals having not fewer than 100 beds.

name: Group C hospitals
description: General hospitals having fewer than 100 beds.

name: Group D hospitals
description: Hospitals that treat patients suffering from cancer, that undertake research with respect to the causes and treatment of cancer and that provide facilities for the instruction of medical students.

name: Group E hospitals
description: General rehabilitation hospitals.

name: Group F hospitals
description: Hospitals for chronic patients having not fewer than 200 beds but not including Group R hospitals.

name: Group G hospitals
description: Hospitals for chronic patients having fewer than 200 beds but not including Group R hospitals or Chronic Patients < 200 beds.

name: Group H hospitals
description: Psychiatric hospitals providing facilities for giving instruction to medical students of any university.

name: Group I hospitals
description: Hospitals designated by the Minister to provide special rehabilitation services for disabled persons in a region of Ontario specified by the Minister for each hospital.

name: Group J hospitals
description: Hospitals designated by the Minister to provide special rehabilitation services for disabled persons in a region of Ontario specified by the Minister for each hospital.

name: Group K hospitals
description: Separate organized facilities approved as such by the Minister, to provide local diagnostic and treatment services in a community or district to handicapped or disabled individuals requiring restorative and adjustive services in an integrated and co-ordinated program.

name: Group L hospitals
description: Hospitals for the treatment of patients suffering from alcoholism and drug addiction and providing facilities for giving instruction to medical students of any university as evidenced by a written agreement between the hospital and the university with which it is affiliated.

volunteer_services: {'description': 'The mission of the Volunteer Services Department is to provide quality support services to our members, visitors, and staff in the Kaiser Permanente San Bernardino County service area and to our local communities. Our volunteers include men and women 16 years of age and older, and represent the culturally diverse community which forms our customer service area. Many volunteers are Kaiser Permanente members, and all share a strong desire to help and a willingness to serve where they are most needed. Volunteers are selected, placed and trained based on their skills, abilities, and service orientation without regard for race, religion, national origin, color, ethnicity, gender, sexual preference or medical condition. Volunteers between the ages of 16 and 17 years old are asked to provide two letters of recommendation (from non-family members) with their applications.', 'contact': {'name': 'Deborah Osborne', 'email': 'Deborah.A.Osborne@kp.org', 'title': 'Director of Volunteer Services'}, 'locations': [{'city': 'Fontana', 'address': '9961 Sierra Ave, Fontana, CA 92316', 'phone': '909-302-7180'}, {'city': 'Ontario', 'address': '2295 S Vineyard Ave, Ontario CA 91761', 'phone': '909-302-7180'}]}

name: Statistics Canada data search
description: Free socioeconomic statistics from Statistics Canada. Contains statistics on health topics including disability, disease, life expectancy, health care services and more.

name: ODESI: Survey and Polling data
description: Source of individual-level microdata from Statistics Canada surveys. Includes resources such as the Canadian Community Health Survey, Health Statistics at a Glance, National Population Health Survey, and Canada Fitness Survey. Also contains a collection of public opinion polls which sometimes ask health questions. For guidance searching ODESI, consult this guide about ODESI.

name: Canadian census data via CHASS
description: Contains census statistics by geographic areas for census years from 1961 to the present.

name: Health in Canada
description: Statistics Canada webpage devoted to health statistics.

name: Statistics Canada website
description: Use the search box on the Statistics Canada website to find general information, analysis and reports as well as links to census and other statistics. On this website you can also locate official documentation about Statistics Canada's many surveys, describing methodology and data quality.

name: Public Health Agency of Canada
description: The Public Health Agency of Canada's website provides statistics and reports on a range of infectious and chronic diseases, drawing on data from Statistics Canada surveys as well as administrative data from organizations like CIHI.

name: First Nations Information Governance Centre (FNIGC) - Data Online
description: Includes statistics from the First Nations Regional Health Survey and the First Nations Regional Early Childhood, Education and Employment Survey.
